cues: Hold, Easy Pose, find your seat with hands on thighs and spine tall.
Close your eyes if that feels good. Arrive here.
steps.adjustment: Let your sit bones ground evenly. Soften the space between the eyebrows.

cues: Three cleansing breaths — big inhale through your nose, open the mouth and sigh it out.
Again, inhale deeply... exhale, let it go.
Last one — inhale fill up... exhale release everything.
steps.adjustment: Let your exhale be audible — release from the back of the throat.

cues: Settle into a count. Inhale for 4... exhale slow and steady for 7.
Three rounds here. Let your exhale be longer than your inhale.
Notice the mind start to quiet as your breath lengthens.
steps.adjustment: If the count feels forced, shorten it. your breath should never be strained.

cues: To root is to trust the ground beneath you — your mat, your breath, your body you already have.
To rise is not to escape, but to grow from what holds you.
Today we practice both. We root down through your feet, your sit bones, the steady exhale. And we rise through your spine, your inhale, the quiet courage of showing up.
Let this be enough. You are already here.
steps.adjustment: Let this land wherever it needs to.

cues: Exhale, Child's Pose, knees wide, big toes touch. Walk your hands forward and let your forehead find your mat.
Melt your heart down with every exhale. 5 slow breaths.
steps.adjustment: Let your hips be heavier than your shoulders. No effort in your neck.

cues: Hold, Table Top, stack your wrists under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Spread your fingers wide and press the earth away.
steps.adjustment: Distribute weight evenly between hands and knees. Gaze between the thumbs.

cues: Inhale, Cat Cow, drop your belly and lift your gaze.
Exhale, round your spine and tuck your chin. 3 rounds, let your breath move you.
steps.adjustment: Close your eyes if you can. Let your movement come from your spine, not your neck.

cues: Exhale, Downward Dog, tuck your toes and lift your hips high. Pedal your feet and shake your head. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Bend your knees as much as you need. The priority is length in your spine.

cues: Inhale, Plank, roll forward to your wrists. One long line from crown to heels, shoulders over wrists.
steps.adjustment: Fire up your quads. Hips in line with your shoulders — not piking up.

cues: Inhale, Cobra, lower to your belly and peel your chest up. Keep your elbows bent and shoulders rolling back.
steps.adjustment: Keep the tops of your feet pressing down. Elbows stay close to your ribs.

cues: Exhale, Downward Dog, press back and lift your hips. 3 full breaths.
steps.adjustment: Let your heels be heavy, even if they don't touch. Relax your neck completely.

cues: Hold, Dangling Pose, walk your feet to the top of your mat. Soft bend in your knees, let your head hang heavy.
steps.adjustment: Soften behind your knees. Let your weight of your head decompress your spine.

cues: Inhale, Halfway Lift, hands to your shins and find a flat back. Long spine, gaze past your mat.
steps.adjustment: Draw your shoulder blades toward each other. Neck stays long, gaze just past your mat.

cues: Inhale, Mountain Pose, rise all the way to standing. Feet together, feel the ground under your feet.
steps.adjustment: Root through all four corners of your feet. Lift your kneecaps gently.

cues: Hold, Mountain Pose, feet together, palms open. Take one full breath before you begin.
steps.adjustment: Ground evenly through both feet. Let your shoulders melt away from your ears.

cues: Inhale, Volcano Pose, sweep your arms overhead. Palms face each other, ribs knit in.
steps.adjustment: Reach through your fingertips, not your shoulders. Ribs stay knit.

cues: Exhale, Forward Fold, swan dive down and hinge at your hips, belly toward your thighs.
steps.adjustment: Soft bend in your knees is fine. Let your head hang fully.

cues: Inhale, Halfway Lift, hands to your shins and find a flat back. Long spine.
steps.adjustment: Fingertips or palms on your shins — not your knees. Keep your neck long.

cues: Exhale, Chaturanga, plant your hands, step back, and lower halfway — or all the way to your belly.
steps.adjustment: Shoulders stay no lower than elbows. Knees down is always an option.

cues: Inhale, Cobra, peel your chest forward and up. Shoulders roll back and down.
steps.adjustment: Lighten your hands — let the back do the lifting. Broaden across your collarbones.

cues: Exhale, Downward Dog, press back and find length through your spine. 5 breaths here.
steps.adjustment: Spin your inner thighs toward the ceiling. Let your breath fill the side ribs.

cues: Inhale, Halfway Lift, step or float to the top.
steps.adjustment: Engage your core as you step forward. Land light.

cues: Exhale, Forward Fold, let your head hang heavy.
steps.adjustment: Relax your jaw. Let the fold happen from your hip crease.

cues: Inhale, Volcano Pose, rise all the way up with arms overhead.
steps.adjustment: Root down through your feet as you rise. Arms track alongside your ears.

cues: Exhale, Mountain Pose, hands to heart. That's one.
steps.adjustment: Soften the thumbs toward your sternum. Feel the heartbeat.

cues: Inhale, Volcano Pose, arms rise. Let your breath lead your movement.
steps.adjustment: Reach tall without gripping your shoulders. Let your breath initiate your movement.

cues: Exhale, Forward Fold, belly draws toward your thighs.
steps.adjustment: Weight shifts slightly forward into the balls of your feet as you fold.

cues: Inhale, Halfway Lift, flat back.
steps.adjustment: Pull your chest forward, not up. Create length between your pelvis and your crown.

cues: Exhale, Chaturanga, elbows hug your ribs.
steps.adjustment: Elbows shoot straight back, not wide. Take your knees down if you need to.

cues: Inhale, Cobra, chest peels up and shoulders roll back.
steps.adjustment: Roll your shoulders back before you lift. Let your inhale do the work.

cues: Exhale, Downward Dog, 3 breaths here.
steps.adjustment: Press the index finger and thumb firmly into your mat. Relax your neck.

cues: Inhale, Halfway Lift, step or float forward.
steps.adjustment: Use your core to draw your feet forward. Light hands, strong center.

cues: Exhale, Forward Fold, release your head.
steps.adjustment: Surrender here. Nothing to fix, just fold.

cues: Inhale, Volcano Pose, rise up with arms overhead.
steps.adjustment: Strong legs, soft face. Rise with intention.

cues: Exhale, Mountain Pose, hands to heart. Two down.
steps.adjustment: Pause at the top. Feel the echo of your movement before you go again.

cues: Inhale, Volcano Pose, last round — move with your breath.
steps.adjustment: Match the pace of your arms to the pace of your inhale.

cues: Exhale, Forward Fold, hinge forward.
steps.adjustment: Exhale everything — belly draws in and up as you fold.

cues: Inhale, Halfway Lift, flat back and long spine.
steps.adjustment: Clean lines. Flat back, strong core, steady gaze.

cues: Exhale, Chaturanga, you know the way.
steps.adjustment: Body stays in one long plank as you lower. Control the descent.

cues: Inhale, Upward Dog, press the tops of your feet down and lift your chest. Thighs lift off the mat.
steps.adjustment: Press the tops of your feet down firmly. Thighs lift away from your mat.

cues: Exhale, Downward Dog, 5 steady breaths. You're warm now.
steps.adjustment: Send your breath into any tight spots. You're warm now — let it show.

cues: Inhale, Halfway Lift, step or float to the top.
steps.adjustment: Trust your legs. Step forward with control.

cues: Exhale, Forward Fold, head heavy.
steps.adjustment: Let go of effort. Just release.

cues: Inhale, Volcano Pose, rise tall.
steps.adjustment: Rise like you mean it. Full breath, full body.

cues: Exhale, Mountain Pose, hands to heart. Three complete rounds.
steps.adjustment: Three rounds complete. Notice how your body has shifted.

cues: Inhale, Chair Pose, bend your knees and sit your hips back. Arms reach overhead, weight in your heels.
steps.adjustment: Draw the low belly in and up. Soften your shoulders away from your ears.

cues: Exhale, Forward Fold, straighten your legs as you go.
steps.adjustment: Keep your knees soft as you straighten. Fold from your hips, not the waist.

cues: Inhale, Halfway Lift, flat back.
steps.adjustment: Shoulders draw away from your ears. Gaze a few inches past your fingertips.

cues: Exhale, Chaturanga, lower with control.
steps.adjustment: Lower with control. Elbows stay tight to your body.

cues: Inhale, Upward Dog, press through the tops of your feet and lift your chest.
steps.adjustment: Tops of your feet press down. Legs engage to lift your thighs off your mat.

cues: Exhale, Downward Dog, press the earth away and breathe.
steps.adjustment: Even out your weight between both hands. Let your head hang between your arms.

cues: Hold, Warrior One, step your right foot forward and spin your back heel down. Hips square, arms reach overhead and shoulders soften.
steps.adjustment: Front knee stacks over your ankle. Back foot is at about a 45-degree angle.

cues: Hold, Warrior Two, open your hips and arms wide. Gaze past your front fingertips. Sink a little deeper into your front knee.
steps.adjustment: Front knee tracks toward the pinky toe. Shoulders stack over your hips.

cues: Inhale, Reverse Warrior, flip your front palm and reach your right arm back. Big stretch through your right side body.
steps.adjustment: Keep your front knee bent as you reach back. Side body stays long.

cues: Cartwheel your hands to your mat. Step back, Chaturanga, Up Dog, Down Dog.
steps.adjustment: Let your breath lead the transition. Move with purpose, not speed.

cues: Hold, Warrior One, left foot steps forward, back heel down. Square your hips and lift your arms.
steps.adjustment: Front knee stacks over your ankle. Back foot is at about a 45-degree angle.

cues: Hold, Warrior Two, open to Warrior Two — left side. Arms wide, gaze past your left fingertips.
steps.adjustment: Front knee tracks toward the pinky toe. Shoulders stack over your hips.

cues: Inhale, Reverse Warrior, flip your front palm and reach back. Big stretch through your left side body.
steps.adjustment: Keep depth in your front knee. Let the back hand slide, don't grip.

cues: Hands to your mat, step back. Vinyasa — Chaturanga, Up Dog, Down Dog.
steps.adjustment: Smooth transitions. No rushing — the vinyasa is the practice too.

cues: Inhale, Chair Pose, step to the top, bend your knees, and reach your arms up. Breathe into it.
steps.adjustment: Shift your weight back into your heels. Chest stays lifted.

cues: Exhale, Forward Fold, release forward.
steps.adjustment: Empty the lungs completely as you fold. Make space for the next inhale.

cues: Inhale, Halfway Lift, flat back.
steps.adjustment: Even a quick halfway lift deserves a long spine. Don't skip the form.

cues: Exhale, vinyasa through.
steps.adjustment: Move at the pace of your breath, not the pace of the room.

cues: Hold, Warrior One, right foot forward. You know this shape — breathe into it.
steps.adjustment: Sink deeper into your front knee. Let your hip flexors open.

cues: Hold, Warrior Two, open wide. Let your breath do the work.
steps.adjustment: Relax the tops of your shoulders. Energy reaches through both fingertips equally.

cues: Inhale, Reverse Warrior, reach and breathe.
steps.adjustment: Soften the effort. Keep the depth, lose the tension.

cues: Vinyasa through to Down Dog.
steps.adjustment: Each vinyasa is a reset. Arrive fresh in the next shape.

cues: Hold, Warrior One, left foot forward. Arms rise and hips square.
steps.adjustment: Front thigh works toward parallel. Back leg stays strong and straight.

cues: Hold, Warrior Two, gaze steady, breath steady.
steps.adjustment: Widen through your collarbones. Let your breath be steady and full.

cues: Inhale, Reverse Warrior, last breath here — reach back.
steps.adjustment: Keep your back leg engaged. Let the reach come from your side body.

cues: Vinyasa. Meet me in Down Dog.
steps.adjustment: Smooth it out. Less effort, same intention.

cues: Inhale, Revolved Chair, step to the top and bend your knees. Exhale, hands at heart and twist left — right elbow outside your left knee.
steps.adjustment: Keep your knees even. Press the palms together to deepen the twist.

cues: Hold, Revolved Chair, come back through center. Exhale, twist to the right — left elbow outside your right knee. Breathe into the space you create.
steps.adjustment: Keep your knees even. Press the palms together to deepen the twist.

cues: Hold, Yogi Toe Lock, straighten your legs and fold forward. Grab your big toes with your peace fingers and lengthen on your inhale, fold deeper on the exhale.
steps.adjustment: Pull up on your toes as you fold to engage the arches. Elbows bend wide.

cues: Hold, Gorilla Pose, slide your hands under your feet with palms up. Let the weight of your head be heavy and breathe into the backs of your legs.
steps.adjustment: Step on the palms — let your wrists get some love. Soft knees if needed.

cues: Release your hands, plant the palms. Step back — vinyasa through.
steps.adjustment: Use your exhale to power the step back. Keep your core engaged through the transition.

cues: Exhale, Knee to Nose, from Downward Dog inhale your right leg high. Exhale, round your spine and draw your right knee toward your nose. Belly draws in.
steps.adjustment: Dome your upper back as your knee draws in. Shoulders stay over your wrists.

cues: Inhale, Low Lunge Twist, step your right foot through and drop your back knee. Rise up, then exhale and twist right — left hand to earth, right arm to the sky.
steps.adjustment: Keep your front knee over your ankle. Stack your shoulders.

cues: Hold, Warrior Two, spin your back heel down and rise. Arms wide, gaze past your front fingertips.
steps.adjustment: Front knee tracks toward the pinky toe side of your foot.

cues: Hold, Extended Side Angle, right forearm rests on your right thigh — or right hand to the earth. Left arm reaches overhead, one long line from back foot to fingertips.
steps.adjustment: Roll the top shoulder open. Gaze can be up or straight ahead.

cues: Inhale, Reverse Warrior, flip your front palm and reach back. Breathe into your right side.
steps.adjustment: Keep your front knee tracking forward even as you reach back.

cues: Hold, Half Moon, straighten your front leg and reach your right fingertips to the earth. Stack your hips and lift your left arm to the sky. Find your steady point.
steps.adjustment: Bottom hand about a foot in front of the standing foot. Flex the top foot, engage the top leg.

cues: Hold, Revolved Triangle, lower your left foot and reach your right arm up and back. Keep both legs straight and breathe into your right side body.
steps.adjustment: Hips face the side of your mat. Engage both legs — no locking out.

cues: Cartwheel your hands down. Step back — vinyasa through to Down Dog.
steps.adjustment: Maintain your breath through the transition. Don't hold it.

cues: Exhale, Knee to Nose, inhale your left leg high. Exhale, round your spine and draw your left knee toward your nose. Hollow through your belly.
steps.adjustment: Hollow your belly as you round. Make yourself compact.

cues: Inhale, Low Lunge Twist, step your left foot through and drop your back knee. Rise up, exhale and twist left — right hand to earth, left arm lifts to the sky.
steps.adjustment: Keep your front knee over your ankle. Stack your shoulders.

cues: Hold, Warrior Two, spin your back heel down — left side. Settle into the shape.
steps.adjustment: Front knee tracks toward the pinky toe side of your foot.

cues: Hold, Extended Side Angle, left forearm to your left thigh and right arm reaching long overhead. Long through the right side body.
steps.adjustment: Roll the top shoulder open. One long line from back foot to fingertips.

cues: Inhale, Reverse Warrior, flip your front palm and reach back. Breathe into your left side.
steps.adjustment: Let the reach be easy. The depth in your front knee does the work.

cues: Hold, Half Moon, straighten your front leg and reach your left fingertips to the earth. Stack your hips and lift your right arm to the sky. Steady gaze.
steps.adjustment: Bottom hand about a foot in front of the standing foot. Flex the top foot.

cues: Hold, Revolved Triangle, right foot returns to your mat. Left arm reaches up and back. Breathe into your left side body.
steps.adjustment: Both legs stay active. Ground through the outer edge of your back foot.

cues: Hands to your mat, step back. Vinyasa — meet me in Down Dog.
steps.adjustment: Let your exhale fuel the step back. Steady hands, steady mind.

cues: Hold, Eagle Pose, stand tall at the top of your mat. Right leg wraps over the left, right arm under the left. Sit low and lift your elbows. Find one steady point to focus on.
steps.adjustment: Sink your hips like you're sitting in a chair. Draw your elbows away from your chest.

cues: Hold, One-Legged Mountain, unwind from Eagle. Stand on your left leg with your right knee lifted to hip height. Hands on hips or arms reach up.
steps.adjustment: Standing foot roots down through all four corners. Micro-bend in the standing knee.

cues: Hold, Standing Figure Four, cross your right ankle over your left knee and sit your hips back. Hands at heart center. Breathe into your right hip.
steps.adjustment: Flex your right foot to protect your knee. Keep the standing knee tracking forward.

cues: Hold, Tree Pose, lift your right foot to your inner left thigh or calf — never on your knee. Arms overhead or hands at heart. Root down to rise up.
steps.adjustment: Press your foot and your leg into each other. Keep the standing hip from jutting out.

cues: Hold, Eagle Pose, switch sides — left leg wraps over right, left arm under right. Sink low and lift your elbows.
steps.adjustment: Sink your hips like you're sitting in a chair. Draw your elbows away from your chest.

cues: Hold, One-Legged Mountain, unwind from Eagle. Stand on your right leg with your left knee lifted. Find your center.
steps.adjustment: Soften the standing hip. Let the lifted knee float rather than grip.

cues: Hold, Standing Figure Four, cross your left ankle over your right knee and sit your hips back. Breathe into your left hip. Stay focused.
steps.adjustment: Flex your left foot to protect your knee.

cues: Hold, Tree Pose, left foot to your inner right thigh or calf. Find your drishti. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Press your foot and your leg into each other.

cues: Hold, Pyramid Pose, step your right foot forward and left foot back. Square your hips, then exhale and fold over your front leg. Hands frame your foot or find blocks.
steps.adjustment: Microbend your front knee if hamstrings are tight. Hips stay square.

cues: Hold, Standing Splits, walk your hands forward and shift your weight to your right foot. Left leg lifts as your head hangs. It's not about how high the leg goes.
steps.adjustment: Hips stay level. Flex the lifted foot and engage the inner thigh.

cues: Hold, Warrior Two, step your left foot back and open wide. Arms reach in both directions.
steps.adjustment: Front knee over your ankle. Torso stays centered between your legs.

cues: Hold, Triangle Pose, straighten your front leg and reach your right hand down to your shin, block, or earth. Left arm extends to the sky and chest opens.
steps.adjustment: Engage your front thigh to protect your knee. Stack your shoulders.

cues: Hold, Star Pose, rise up with legs wide and arms wide. Take up space here.
steps.adjustment: Ground through the outer edges of both feet. Spread your fingers wide.

cues: Exhale, Wide-Legged Forward Fold, hands to your hips and hinge forward. Crown of your head reaches toward your mat. 5 deep breaths.
steps.adjustment: Walk your hands between your feet. Elbows bend toward the back of the room.

cues: Hold, Pyramid Pose, walk your hands forward and pivot to face the left side. Fold over your left leg. Breathe into your hamstring.
steps.adjustment: Microbend your front knee if needed. Hips stay square.

cues: Hold, Standing Splits, shift your weight forward onto your left leg. Right leg rises. Relax your neck and breathe.
steps.adjustment: Keep your hips square even as your leg lifts. It's about balance, not height.

cues: Hold, Warrior Two, step your right foot back — left side. Settle in.
steps.adjustment: Front knee over your ankle. Torso centered.

cues: Hold, Triangle Pose, straighten your front leg — left side. Left hand reaches down, right arm extends to the sky.
steps.adjustment: Engage your front thigh. Stack your shoulders.

cues: Inhale, Star Pose, rise up. One breath — feel the expanse.
steps.adjustment: Open your chest. One breath of expansion before you fold.

cues: Exhale, Wide-Legged Forward Fold, let gravity do the work. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Release your crown of your head toward the earth. Soften the inner groins.

cues: Walk your hands forward. Step back to plank — vinyasa to Down Dog. Lower all the way to your belly.
steps.adjustment: Stay connected to your breath as you lower. No crashing.

cues: Inhale, Locust Pose, forehead to your mat and arms alongside your body. On your inhale lift your legs, chest, and arms. Reach your fingertips toward your heels.
steps.adjustment: Reach your sternum forward, not up. Keep the back of your neck long.

cues: Inhale, Bound Locust, interlace your hands behind your back. Lift your chest and open your heart. Squeeze your shoulder blades together, legs stay lifted.
steps.adjustment: Knuckles point toward the ceiling. Broaden across the front of your chest.

cues: Inhale, Bow Pose, bend your knees and reach back to grab your outer ankles. Kick your feet into your hands and lift on your inhale. Breathe wide into your ribs.
steps.adjustment: Keep your knees hip-width, not splaying out. Kick into your hands to lift higher.

cues: Exhale, Child's Pose, release everything and press back. Let your spine neutralize. Breathe into your low back.
steps.adjustment: Breathe into the back body. Let your belly soften toward your thighs.

cues: Exhale, Seated Forward Bend, come to a seat with legs extended. Inhale and lengthen. Exhale and fold forward, head heavy.
steps.adjustment: Sit up on a blanket if your low back rounds. Lead with your chest, not your head.

cues: Exhale, Head to Knee Pose, bend your right knee and place your foot to your inner left thigh. Inhale, lengthen. Exhale, fold over your left leg.
steps.adjustment: Keep the extended leg active — press through your heel. Rotate toward the straight leg.

cues: Exhale, Head to Knee Pose, switch sides — left knee bends. Fold over your right leg. Soften your jaw and let your breath be easy.
steps.adjustment: Relax the grip. Let the fold be a surrender, not a pull.

cues: Exhale, Seated Straddle, open both legs wide and walk your hands forward. Let gravity draw you down. 8 slow breaths.
steps.adjustment: Flex both feet. Walk your hands only as far as your spine stays long.

cues: Hold, Half Pigeon, from Downward Dog bring your right knee behind your right wrist. Walk your hands forward and lower down. Breathe into your right hip. 10 deep breaths.
steps.adjustment: If your hip is off the ground, slide a blanket underneath. Protect your back knee.

cues: Hold, Half Pigeon, press back to Downward Dog and switch sides. Left knee comes forward. Let your hip open at its own pace. 10 deep breaths.
steps.adjustment: Don't force depth. your hip opens on its own timeline.

cues: Inhale, Bridge Pose, feet flat and hip-width, close to your sit bones. Press through your feet and lift your hips. Interlace your hands underneath you and walk your shoulders together.
steps.adjustment: Keep your knees tracking over your ankles, not splaying out. Press evenly through all four corners of your feet.

cues: Inhale, Bridge or Wheel, lower down for round two. Stay with Bridge — or place your hands by your ears and press up into Wheel on your inhale. Stay with what serves you today.
steps.adjustment: In Wheel, straighten your arms fully. Press through your hands and feet evenly.

cues: Hold, Constructive Rest, lower down gently. Feet as wide as your mat, knees falling in. Hands rest on your belly. Let your low back release.
steps.adjustment: Let the whole back body release into the floor. Nothing to hold.

cues: Hold, Happy Baby, draw your knees in and catch the outer edges of your feet. Rock gently side to side.
steps.adjustment: Keep your tailbone reaching toward your mat. Elbows can bend wide.

cues: Exhale, Supine Twist, drop both knees to the right and reach your left arm wide. Gaze over your left shoulder. Let your spine wring out.
steps.adjustment: Both shoulders stay grounded. Knees don't have to touch the floor.

cues: Exhale, Supine Twist, bring your knees back through center and drop to the left. Right arm reaches wide. Breathe into your right side.
steps.adjustment: Let the twist happen in the mid-spine. Keep your belly soft.

cues: Hold, Waterfall, extend your legs straight up and let the blood flow back toward your heart. 10 slow breaths.
steps.adjustment: If your low back lifts, bend your knees slightly. Let your legs feel heavy.

cues: Hold, Savasana, lower your legs and let your arms rest by your sides, palms up. Close your eyes and let every muscle release. Nothing to do. Nowhere to be.
steps.adjustment: Scan from your crown of your head to your toes. Release anything still holding.

cues: Begin to deepen your breath. Wiggle your fingers and toes.
Return to the intention you set at the beginning. Let it land again.
steps.adjustment: There's no rush. Let awareness come back gently.

cues: Hold, Easy Pose, roll to your right side and press yourself up to a seat. Hands to heart center.
Bow your chin toward your fingertips. Thank you for showing up today. Namaste.
steps.adjustment: Honor the practice exactly as it was today. Nothing to change.

name: Julie's Paint and Flow Fundraiser
subtitle: Paint, Sing & Flow: A May Day Offering for Immigrant Defense Project
theme: Collective power, community, internal fire. Fundraiser for Immigrant Defense Project on May Day.

cues: Hold, Easy Pose, find a comfortable seat and let your spine grow tall.
Eyes soft or closed. Let yourself arrive.

Today we gather on May Day — International Workers' Day — a holiday born from the labor movement, from immigrants and workers who fought, organized, and bled for the eight-hour workday, for dignity, for the radical idea that working people deserve rest and joy and beauty. May Day has always belonged to the people. It's a day of solidarity that reminds us that no one is free until we are all free.

We're here to raise funds for the Immigrant Defense Project, an organization that fights for the rights of immigrants facing deportation and detention, that defends people whose lives are being torn apart by an unjust system. IDP doesn't just provide legal defense — they organize, they educate, and they refuse to let anyone be disappeared without a fight.

Today we're weaving three practices together: movement — the body remembering it is powerful. Voice — the throat that has been told to stay quiet learning to sing. And paint — the hand that creates rather than only produces. These are the same tools that movements have always used. Workers sang on picket lines. Communities painted murals on their buildings. Bodies marched in the streets. We're not doing anything new — we're remembering.
steps.adjustment: GROUP MOMENT — opening framing. Let it land wherever it needs to.

cues: Bring fingertips of both hands together in front of the chest or belly — thumb to thumb, index to index, all the way through pinky to pinky. Fingers spread wide, palms apart. This is hakini mudra.

This shape is older than any of us. It's a gesture of gathering — of bringing what is scattered back together. Notice that your two hands, which usually move independently, are now in conversation. This is the same practice we'll do all day: bringing things that seem separate into relationship. Body and breath. Self and community. You and the person on the mat next to you.
A few natural breaths, just feeling the fingertip contact.
Inhale through the nose for 4 counts. Exhale through the nose for 6 counts.
On each inhale, draw energy in through the fingertips toward the heart. On each exhale, let the shoulders drop, jaw soften, belly release. 8–10 rounds.

Each fingertip touch is a small reminder: nothing in you is meant to be alone. The breath that fills your lungs has been around the world. It has been breathed by people you love and people you'll never meet. It has been breathed by every immigrant who has ever crossed a border looking for safety, for work, for family. We share air. We have always shared air.
Inhale — gathering. Exhale — softening. We're not building anything yet. We're just arriving.
One final deep inhale, hold briefly at the top, long sighing exhale through the mouth. Release the mudra, hands rest on thighs or come to heart.
steps.adjustment: GROUP MOMENT — hakini mudra breathwork. Inhale 4, exhale 6.

cues: Inhale, Seated Cat Cow, drop your belly and lift your gaze. Exhale, round your spine and release your head.
Inhale, Easy Pose Side Bend, reach your right arm overhead and let your left hand ground beside you.
Hold and breathe into the left side of your body. Switch sides after 3 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side first, then left.

cues: Inhale, Revolved Easy Pose, lengthen through your crown.
Exhale, twist to the right — right hand behind you, left hand to your right knee. 3 breaths, then switch to the left.
steps.adjustment: Right first, then left.

cues: Hold, Table Top, stack your wrists under your shoulders and your knees under your hips. Spread your fingers wide.
Inhale, Cat Cow, drop your belly and lift your gaze. Exhale, round your spine and tuck your chin. 3 slow rounds.
Exhale, Thread the Needle, slide your right arm under the left and let your right cheek rest on the mat. 5 breaths.
Return to tabletop, then thread your left arm under. 5 breaths on this side.
steps.adjustment: Right first, then left.

cues: Exhale, Child's Pose, press your hips back toward your heels and reach your arms long.
Let your forehead soften to the mat. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: The collective bow.

cues: Exhale, Puppy Pose, walk your hands forward and let your chest melt toward the floor, hips stacked over knees.
Exhale, Downward Dog, tuck your toes and lift your hips. Pedal through your heels and lengthen your spine.
Inhale, Halfway Lift, walk to the top of your mat and find a flat back.
Exhale, Forward Fold, let your head hang heavy.
Inhale, Mountain Pose, roll up slowly and sweep your arms wide overhead.
Feel the ground beneath you.
3 slow rounds of breath-with-movement Sun A: Mountain → forward fold → halfway lift → fold → mountain. No jump backs yet. Just the breath.
Notice the person breathing next to you. You are not flowing alone.
steps.adjustment: 3 slow rounds. Breath is the guide.

cues: Inhale, Mountain Pose, sweep your arms overhead and press your feet into the earth.
Exhale, Forward Fold, hinge from your hips and let your head hang heavy.
Inhale, Halfway Lift, find a flat back and lengthen through your crown.
Exhale, Chaturanga, step or float back to plank and lower with your elbows grazing the ribs.
Inhale, Upward Dog, press the tops of your feet down and lift your chest.
Exhale, Downward Dog, press the earth away and find length through your spine. 3 breaths.
Inhale, Three-Legged Dog, lift your right leg high and square your hips.
steps.adjustment: Right side.

cues: Exhale, Knee to Elbow, draw your right knee toward your right elbow and round through your spine.
Inhale, Three-Legged Dog, extend your right leg long and open through your hip.
Exhale, Lizard Pose, step your right foot outside your right hand and sink your hips toward the mat. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side. Low to the ground, close to the source.

cues: Exhale, Garland Pose, step your feet together and lower into a deep squat with your hands at heart center.
Heels can lift or rest on a support. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Malasana — garland pose. Heels lifted or supported.

cues: Inhale, Star Pose, step wide and reach your arms overhead. Take up every inch of space. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Take up space.

cues: Forward fold, halfway lift, step back to plank. Vinyasa — chaturanga, upward dog, downward dog.
steps.adjustment: Repeat Sun A on left side (steps 16–28), then return to downward dog.

cues: Inhale, Three-Legged Dog, lift your right leg from downward dog and square your hips.
steps.adjustment: Right side first.

cues: Exhale, Lizard Pose, step your right foot outside your right hand. Keep your hips low. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side.

cues: Exhale, Wide-Legged Forward Fold, step your feet wide and fold forward with a long spine. 5 breaths.
Inhale, Star Pose, rise tall with your arms wide and feel the length through your fingertips. 3 breaths.
Hold, Triangle Pose, turn your right toes out and reach long through your right arm. Stack your left arm toward the sky. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side.

cues: Inhale, Half Moon, bend your front knee and shift your weight forward as your left leg lifts. Right hand to the floor or a block. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side.

cues: Exhale, Skandasana, bend your right knee deeply and extend your left leg long beside you. 3 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side. Wide squat lateral lunge — weight over right knee.

cues: Hold, Sky Archer, shift your weight to the left side and reach long through both arms like drawing back a bow. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Left side. Lateral lunge over left leg, right leg extended.

cues: Inhale, Low Lunge, sweep your arms overhead and sink low. Exhale, hands frame your front foot. 3 rounds.
steps.adjustment: Right foot forward. 3 rounds breath-with-movement.

cues: Exhale, Half Splits, straighten your front leg and fold forward over it, keeping a long spine. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side. Ardha hanumanasana.

cues: Bend back into low lunge, plant hands, step back to plank. Vinyasa — chaturanga, upward dog, downward dog.
steps.adjustment: Repeat Sun B on left side (steps 29–39), then return to downward dog.

cues: Inhale, Mountain Pose, walk or hop to the top of your mat and roll up to stand.
Step wide, lower into goddess. Twist the torso and reach the opposite hand toward the opposite foot, rise and switch sides. 10 reps.
This is your heat peak — the fire we've been building.
steps.adjustment: Shiva squat / side-to-side squat thrusters. 10 reps.

cues: Hold, Goddess Pose, step wide and bend your knees. Lift your arms to cactus — elbows wide, palms forward. Pulse down 8 times.
steps.adjustment: Cactus arms, 8 pulses.

cues: Hold, Goddess Pose, drop your right elbow toward your left knee. Alternate sides for 8 reps.
steps.adjustment: Alternating sides, 8 reps.

cues: Hold, Skandasana, shift side to side through a low squat, letting the breath guide you. 6 reps.
steps.adjustment: 6 reps side to side.

cues: Hold, Eagle Arms, stand wide with soft knees. Wrap your right arm under your left and lift your elbows. 5 breaths, then switch sides.
steps.adjustment: Horse stance with eagle arms — both sides.

cues: Vinyasa to cool the surface — chaturanga, upward dog, downward dog.
Walk to the top of the mat. Rise to a wide stance. One hand on belly, one on heart.
10 sharp 'HA!' exhales — loud, releasing sound from the belly. All together.
Pause. Inhale deeply.
steps.adjustment: GROUP MOMENT — voice integration. Loud, collective, from the belly.

cues: One long collective 'AHHHH' — hold together for 30 seconds. Everyone at once.
Pause. One natural breath.
One more long collective 'AHHHH' — let it get louder, fuller.
This is the sound of breaking silence. The sound IDP's clients are too often not allowed to make. We make it for them and with them.
Hands to heart. One natural breath. Step feet together at the top of the mat.
steps.adjustment: GROUP MOMENT — 30-second collective tone, twice.

cues: Hold, Mountain Pose, root through both feet and let your breath settle. 3 breaths.
Hold, Tree Pose, root your left foot into the earth and lift your right foot to your inner calf or thigh. Hands at heart.
steps.adjustment: Right side first.

cues: Hold, Standing Figure Four, cross your right ankle over your left thigh and sit your hips back. Hands at heart. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side.

cues: Inhale, Chair Pose, sweep your arms overhead and sit your hips back toward an invisible seat. 5 breaths.
Hold, Warrior Three, shift your weight to your left foot and hinge forward as your right leg lifts behind you. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side — balanced on left foot.

cues: Exhale, Standing Splits, hinge deeper and lift your right leg as high as it will go. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side.

cues: Hold, Dancer Pose, bend your standing knee, catch your right foot behind you, and press your foot into your hand as you rise and extend. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: PEAK POSE — right side. Natarajasana.

cues: Hold, Warrior Two, step your right foot back and bend your front knee over your ankle. Arms reach long in both directions. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side.

cues: Inhale, Reverse Warrior, flip your front palm and arc your right arm up and back. 3 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side.

cues: Hold, Extended Side Angle, lower your right forearm to your thigh and reach your left arm overhead. Long through the left side body. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side.

cues: Hold, Triangle Pose, straighten your front leg and reach your right hand down as your left arm extends toward the sky. 5 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Right side.

cues: Cartwheel hands down, step back to plank. Vinyasa — chaturanga, upward dog, downward dog.
steps.adjustment: Repeat entire balance sequence on left side (steps 50–60), then return to downward dog.

cues: Have everyone turn to face the center of the room. Step into tree pose — any side, dealer's choice. Branches reaching up and slightly toward the center.
Hold together for 5–8 long breaths.
This is what the immigrant rights movement looks like. Individual roots, individual sky-reaching, but moving as one forest. You are not holding this alone. You have never been holding this alone.
Release tree, hands to heart, slight bow toward the center of the room. Turn back to face the front of your mat.
steps.adjustment: GROUP MOMENT — all face center, hold tree together.

cues: Exhale, Seated Straddle, open your legs wide and walk your hands forward, folding from your hips. 8 breaths.
Exhale, Head to Knee Pose, extend your right leg and fold forward over it with a long spine. 5 breaths, then switch sides.
steps.adjustment: Right then left, 5 breaths each.

cues: Exhale, Seated Forward Bend, extend both legs and fold forward, reaching through your feet. 8 breaths.
Hold, Reclined Pigeon, cross your right ankle over your left knee, flex your right foot, and draw both legs toward your chest. 8 breaths, then switch sides.
steps.adjustment: Right then left, 8 breaths each. Supta kapotasana.

cues: Hold, Happy Baby, catch the outer edges of your feet and let your spine melt into the mat. Rock gently. 5 breaths.
Exhale, Supine Twist, stack your knees to the left and open your arms wide. Let your spine release. 5 breaths, then switch sides.
steps.adjustment: Right then left, 5 breaths each.

cues: Inhale, Bridge Pose, press through your feet and lift your hips toward the sky. 5 breaths. Lower and repeat.
steps.adjustment: 2 rounds, 5 breaths each.

cues: Inhale, Fish Pose, place support under your shoulder blades and let your chest open toward the sky. Throat softens. 8 breaths.
steps.adjustment: Supported with bolster or block under shoulder blades, optional.

cues: Roll to one side, press up to a comfortable seat. Spine tall.
Bring hands back into hakini mudra — fingertips touching, the same shape we started in.

We started this practice gathering ourselves. Now we return to that gesture, but with a body that has been through something. The fire is built. The voice has cracked open. The hands are ready to make.
Inhale through nose for 4. Hold gently at the top for 4. Sigh out through the mouth, audibly. 4 rounds.
steps.adjustment: Inhale 4, hold 4, sigh out. 4 rounds.

cues: The fire you built today doesn't stay on this mat. It walks out with you. Every dollar raised today goes to IDP, to lawyers and organizers fighting in courts and on streets. Your breath was a contribution. Your sweat was a contribution. Your voice and your painting are contributions. This is what mutual aid looks like when it has a body.
Take a moment to silently dedicate your practice to one person — named or unnamed — who is impacted by the immigration system. Hold them in your mind. Hold them in your hands.
One final breath together. Bow forward.
Hand off to the song leader, or invite students to begin painting.
steps.adjustment: GROUP MOMENT — closing dedication. Bow. Hand off to song leader.
